rationale: The second row has an empty LockOnReset set and was unlocked before PowerCycle. The source says an empty set means ReadLocked and WriteLocked do not change on any reset, so returning the row still unlocked is supported.

rationale: The second row's LockOnReset is empty and its WriteLocked value was false before PowerCycle. Empty LockOnReset preserves the locked columns through reset, so returning WriteLocked true is not supported.

label: FAIL
rationale: The first range has WriteLockEnabled true and LockOnReset containing Power Cycle, so after PowerCycle its WriteLocked state is true. The WriteLocked rule says the TPer shall not allow user-data writes when write locking is enabled and WriteLocked is true, so a successful write to that range is non-compliant.

rationale: The second range has WriteLockEnabled true, WriteLocked false, and empty LockOnReset, so PowerCycle does not change its unlocked write state. A write to LBAs covered by that row is therefore allowed.

label: FAIL
rationale: The second range remains write-unlocked because its LockOnReset set is empty. The WriteLocked rule says writes are allowed when WriteLockEnabled is true and WriteLocked is false, so rejecting the write is unsupported by the supplied evidence.
concerns: The evidence is about whether the write must be allowed, not about the exact error code a device would choose for an unrelated host I/O failure; within this trajectory no unrelated failure condition is shown.

label: PASS
rationale: After PowerCycle, at least one range has WriteLockEnabled true and WriteLocked true. The Level 0 Locking Feature Locked bit must be one if any enabled locking range is read-locked or write-locked, so Locked=1 is supported.

label: FAIL
rationale: The first range is write-lock enabled and becomes WriteLocked after PowerCycle because LockOnReset includes Power Cycle. Since one enabled range is locked, the Level 0 Locked bit must be one, not zero.

label: PASS
rationale: The only locked row has its WriteLockEnabled column set to false after the reset, and ReadLockEnabled was already false. The Level 0 Locked bit depends on enabled read/write locking plus the corresponding locked state, so with the enabled lock cleared the feature should report Locked=0.
concerns: Stored locked cells may remain true, but the cited Level 0 definition gates them on ReadLockEnabled or WriteLockEnabled being true.

label: FAIL
rationale: After WriteLockEnabled is set false on the only locked range, there is no enabled range whose corresponding lock state contributes to the Level 0 Locked bit. Reporting Locked=1 is therefore unsupported.
concerns: This assumes no other range in the trajectory remains enabled and locked; the second row has empty LockOnReset and remains unlocked.
